name: toll-bridge-house
summary: A gatehouse beside a bridge or ford. Lowerable bar across the road, fee-table, a guard or two, a shelter from the weather. Part building, part roadside structure.

primary_palette.note: house beside the road, barrier across — classic checkpoint
barrier front-and-center, guards' brazier on one side
busy day — a cart waits at the bar, goods being inspected

story_prompts: official toll — reasonable fee, bored guards, paperwork posted
bandit shakedown — the 'guards' are thieves, the toll is robbery with extra steps
closed — barrier down, no one in the house, a notice nailed to the door
the bridge behind is damaged — the toll-keeper is warning travelers off, not taking coin
a noble's vanity toll — exorbitant fee, liveried guards, the house flies a crest

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: outdoor road — daylit, brazier and lantern for night traffic

anti_patterns: the toll house is a structure within a roadside space, not a full building interior
the road must be clearly readable — do not decorate so heavily it disappears
barrier is perpendicular to the road, not parallel — it blocks passage
keep guards' kit at the fire/brazier, not on the road itself
